summary: Full E2E test of DeepSeek roleplay app — 8/8 backend pytest cases PASS, all critical frontend flows PASS on both desktop (1920x1080) and mobile (390x844). DeepSeek V4 Flash live API responded in 1-4s consistently. localStorage persistence verified after refresh and cross-page nav. Export-all downloads valid JSON with all 4 keys.

backend: POST /api/chat with Kira system prompt -> in-character reply (>10 chars)
POST /api/chat with malformed payload -> 422 validation error
POST /api/chat/extract-memories -> non-empty JSON list of memory strings
frontend: Gallery shows 3 seed characters (Kira, Isolde, Rook) with images/taglines/tags
Search filter narrows to 1 card on 'kira'
Nav buttons present: nav-profile, nav-settings, create-character-button, export-all-button
Open chat seed-kira -> opening message rendered immediately from initialMessage
Edit / Regenerate / Delete action buttons all visible without hover
Send message -> DeepSeek reply rendered in ~3s
Scene sheet opens via theater icon, pre-filled with character.sceneDefault, fields persist
Memory sheet opens via brain icon; add memory + remove memory work
Regenerate last AI msg -> swipe-prev / swipe-next appear (variant 2/2)
Refresh page -> messages + memory + scene restored from localStorage
Create character form -> Save -> lands on /chat/:id with new char's initial msg
Edit character form pre-fills name; delete-character-button present and works (confirm dialog auto-accepted)
Settings page has 5 sliders (creativity, romanticism, emotionalIntensity, maxTokens, shortHistory) + reset button
Delete message: 3 msgs -> 2 msgs, target removed from DOM
Edit user message -> trims subsequent + auto-regenerates fresh AI reply with updated content
Export All button downloads roleplay-export-YYYY-MM-DD.json with characters/chats/profile/settings
Mobile 390x844: gallery renders, composer visible at y=771 within viewport, scene+memory sheets open correctly

critical_code_review_comments: server.py: clean, thin proxy (~300 lines), good separation. No issues.
DEEPSEEK_API_KEY is read at import time — restart needed if key changes; acceptable for this app.
Frontend: testids comprehensive on all interactive elements. Excellent.

Minor: data-testid='message-composer' shares the 'message-' prefix used by chat messages; if a future test uses [data-testid^="message-"] it will accidentally match the composer textarea. Consider renaming to 'composer-input' for clarity. Non-blocking.
test_credentials: Not applicable — app is localStorage-only with no auth.
seed_data_creation: App auto-seeds 3 characters (seed-kira, seed-isolde, seed-rook) into localStorage on first load via ensureSeed(). Verified seeding works on cleared storage.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All major flows verified end-to-end against live DeepSeek V4 Flash API (responses arrive in 1-4s, well under the 30s budget). When asserting message counts via [data-testid^="message-"], remember that 'message-composer' also matches; use [data-testid^="message-m_"] or filter to actual chat bubbles. localStorage is the only persistence — clear it between runs for fresh state. Note that previously created TEST_ characters may linger in localStorage between runs.
rca_of_the_issue: No issues found. Initial selector ambiguity in my first delete-test was a test bug (matched message-composer), confirmed working on focused retest.
